rationale: The answer rejects an even-handed 'both sides' synthesis and establishes a dominant judgment: old-code maintainability problems justify evidence-driven governance and usually gradual replacement, not a whole-backend rewrite. It demotes rewrite and anti-rewrite absolutism into constraints, names decision evidence and migration controls, and treats full rewrite as a last option under specific conditions. This satisfies the aspect-arbitration expectation without collapsing into balanced mush.
